title: [VerifToSMT] Add meaningful name prefixes to BMC symbolic constants
body:
This adds descriptive name prefixes to `smt.declare_fun` ops created during the VerifToSMT BMC lowering. Previously, all symbolic constants were unnamed, resulting in auto-generated Z3 names (`x!0`, `x!1`, etc.) that made `--print-solver-output` model dumps unreadable.

Free inputs are now named `input_N` and uninitialized registers `reg_N`, which flow through to `Z3_mk_fresh_const` as prefixes. This makes counter-example models from `--print-solver-output` immediately interpretable.
### Example

A counter that asserts it never reaches 5:
```mlir
hw.module @counter(in %clk: !seq.clock, in %data_in: i8, out out: i8) {
%reg = seq.compreg %next, %clk : i8
%c1 = hw.constant 1 : i8
%next = comb.add %reg, %c1 : i8
%c5 = hw.constant 5 : i8
%not_five = comb.icmp ne %reg, %c5 : i8
verif.assert %not_five : i1
hw.output %reg : i8
}
```

**Before** (`circt-bmc --print-solver-output`):
```
(declare-fun !1 () (_ BitVec 8))
(assert (not (= (ite (distinct !1 #x05) #b1 #b0) #b1)))
--- Model ---
!1 -> #x05
```

**After:**
```
(declare-fun reg_0!1 () (_ BitVec 8))
(assert (not (= (ite (distinct reg_0!1 #x05) #b1 #b0) #b1)))
--- Model ---
reg_0!1 -> #x05
```

`!1` becomes `reg_0!1` — immediately identifiable as the register
value that hit the assertion violation at value 5.
